Chief Complaint: Hit on left frontal area with swelling and abrasion since 07/29 at night.
Present illness: Background: This is a 61-year-old male with underlying diseases of cardiac valve disease under Pradaxa treatment.
Last Admission: He was admitted to the General Surgery department from 2026-04-15 to 2026-05-09 for small bowel obstruction, where he underwent jejunocolostomy and enterolysis on 2026-04-23.

Present Illness: According to the patient, the chief complaint was a fall resulting in a hit to the left frontal area on 07/29 at night. He stood up but fell down, sustaining a 0.7cm mild abrasion in the left brow. This morning, he accidentally found swelling in the left frontal area while exercising, accompanied by ecchymosis in bilateral knees. Due to the above mentioned symptoms, the patient visited our ER. Physical examination revealed clear consciousness, normal visual acuity, supple neck without rigidity, and soft abdomen without peritoneal signs. Extremities showed full range of motion in knees and full muscle power in all four limbs.
Past History: Medical: Cardiac valve disease under Pradaxa treatment, small bowel obstruction.
Surgical: Cardiac valve surgery, jejunocolostomy, enterolysis.
Impression: Head injury with left frontal contusion, abrasion, and swelling, and bilateral knee ecchymosis post fall, in a patient with a history of cardiac valve surgery under Pradaxa.
